title: tests: fix diff cmd test on macOS HFS+, fixes #8860
body: If we touch too quickly after file creation, it would not noticably update the timestamp.

step_plan.summary: Make `borg diff`’s timestamp-based test (`test_time_diffs`) reliable on macOS filesystems with coarse timestamp granularity by ensuring mtime/ctime have enough time to advance before operations that are expected to change them.

steps.operation: Stabilize timestamp-dependent operations by enforcing a measurable mtime/ctime delta before touch/chmod expectations in the diff test (add/adjust wait/sleep in the test sequence, with Darwin/HFS+ granularity in mind).
steps.reason: On macOS (notably HFS+ and sometimes APFS), timestamps can have coarse granularity and may not visibly update if a file is modified too soon after creation; this makes `test_time_diffs` flaky because it assumes immediate mtime/ctime changes will be detectable by `borg diff`. Ensuring a detectable time delta before the operations under test makes the assertions deterministic without changing the product code.
steps.side_effects: May slightly increase test runtime on Darwin (potentially ~1s depending on implementation). If implemented as unconditional sleeps, could slow other platforms; prefer conditional logic for Darwin/Windows only. Any timing change may affect assumptions in nearby assertions (e.g., ordering of create/delete/recreate), so run the full testsuite for `diff_cmd_test.py` across platforms.
